title: auto - letting the type be inferred
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: C++ is statically typed, but auto lets the compiler infer the type from the value.
explanation: When the type is obvious from the right-hand side, auto saves you writing it out. The variable is still strongly typed - auto just works out what that type is.

exercises.question: What type does 'auto count = 10;' give count?
What does auto actually do?
options: infers the type from the value
hints: The variable is still strongly typed.
It just saves you writing the type out.
It infers the type from the value.

exercises.question:
Which keyword lets the compiler work out the type for you?

_ count = 10;
hints: It's the keyword this lesson is about.
